NKJV.1: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, his utterance. This man declared to Ithiel—to Ithiel and Ucal:
NKJV.2: Surely I am more stupid than any man, And do not have the understanding of a man.
NKJV.3: I neither learned wisdom Nor have knowledge of the Holy One.
NKJV.4: Who has ascended into heaven, or descended? Who has gathered the wind in His fists? Who has bound the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name, and what is His Son’s name, If you know?
NKJV.5: Every word of God is pure; He is a shield to those who put their trust in Him.
NKJV.6: Do not add to His words, Lest He rebuke you, and you be found a liar.
NKJV.7: Two things I request of You (Deprive me not before I die):
NKJV.8: Remove falsehood and lies far from me; Give me neither poverty nor riches—  Feed me with the food allotted to me;
NKJV.9: Lest I be full and deny You, And say, “Who is the LORD?” Or lest I be poor and steal, And profane the name of my God.
NKJV.10: Do not malign a servant to his master, Lest he curse you, and you be found guilty.
NKJV.11: There is a generation that curses its father, And does not bless its mother.
NKJV.12: There is a generation that is pure in its own eyes, Yet is not washed from its filthiness.
NKJV.13: There is a generation—oh, how lofty are their eyes! And their eyelids are lifted up.
NKJV.14: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords, And whose fangs are like knives, To devour the poor from off the earth, And the needy from among men.
NKJV.15: The leech has two daughters— Give and Give! There are three things that are never satisfied, Four never say, “Enough!”:
NKJV.16: The grave, The barren womb, The earth that is not satisfied with water— And the fire never says, “Enough!”
NKJV.17: The eye that mocks his father, And scorns obedience to his mother, The ravens of the valley will pick it out, And the young eagles will eat it.
NKJV.18: There are three things which are too wonderful for me, Yes, four which I do not understand:
NKJV.19: The way of an eagle in the air, The way of a serpent on a rock, The way of a ship in the midst of the sea, And the way of a man with a virgin.
NKJV.20: This is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth, And says, “I have done no wickedness.”
NKJV.21: For three things the earth is perturbed, Yes, for four it cannot bear up:
NKJV.22: For a servant when he reigns, A fool when he is filled with food,
NKJV.23: A hateful woman when she is married, And a maidservant who succeeds her mistress.
NKJV.24: There are four things which are little on the earth, But they are exceedingly wise:
NKJV.25: The ants are a people not strong, Yet they prepare their food in the summer;
NKJV.26: The rock badgers are a feeble folk, Yet they make their homes in the crags;
NKJV.27: The locusts have no king, Yet they all advance in ranks;
NKJV.28: The spider skillfully grasps with its hands, And it is in kings’ palaces.
NKJV.29: There are three things which are majestic in pace, Yes, four which are stately in walk:
NKJV.30: A lion, which is mighty among beasts And does not turn away from any;
NKJV.31: A greyhound, A male goat also, And a king whose troops are with him.
NKJV.32: If you have been foolish in exalting yourself, Or if you have devised evil, put your hand on your mouth.
NKJV.33: For as the churning of milk produces butter, And wringing the nose produces blood, So the forcing of wrath produces strife.

ESV.1: The words of Agur son of Jakeh. The oracle.  The man declares, I am weary, O God; I am weary, O God, and worn out.
ESV.2: Surely I am too stupid to be a man. I have not the understanding of a man.
ESV.3: I have not learned wisdom, nor have I knowledge of the Holy One.
ESV.4: Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son's name? Surely you know!
ESV.5: Every word of God proves true; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
ESV.6: Do not add to his words, lest he rebuke you and you be found a liar.
ESV.7: Two things I ask of you; deny them not to me before I die:
ESV.8: Remove far from me falsehood and lying; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that is needful for me,
ESV.9: lest I be full and deny you and say, “Who is the LORD?” or lest I be poor and steal and profane the name of my God.
ESV.10: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you be held guilty.
ESV.11: There are those  who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
ESV.12: There are those who are clean in their own eyes but are not washed of their filth.
ESV.13: There are those — how lofty are their eyes, how high their eyelids lift!
ESV.14: There are those whose teeth are swords, whose fangs are knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, the needy from among mankind.
ESV.15: The leech has two daughters: Give and Give.  Three things are never satisfied; four never say, “Enough”:
ESV.16: Sheol, the barren womb, the land never satisfied with water, and the fire that never says, “Enough.”
ESV.17: The eye that mocks a father and scorns to obey a mother will be picked out by the ravens of the valley and eaten by the vultures.
ESV.18: Three things are too wonderful for me; four I do not understand:
ESV.19: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a serpent on a rock, the way of a ship on the high seas, and the way of a man with a virgin.
ESV.20: This is the way of an adulteress: she eats and wipes her mouth and says, “I have done no wrong.”
ESV.21: Under three things the earth trembles; under four it cannot bear up:
ESV.22: a slave when he becomes king, and a fool when he is filled with food;
ESV.23: an unloved woman when she gets a husband, and a maidservant when she displaces her mistress.
ESV.24: Four things on earth are small, but they are exceedingly wise:
ESV.25: the ants are a people not strong, yet they provide their food in the summer;
ESV.26: the rock badgers are a people not mighty, yet they make their homes in the cliffs;
ESV.27: the locusts have no king, yet all of them march in rank;
ESV.28: the lizard you can take in your hands, yet it is in kings' palaces.
ESV.29: Three things are stately in their tread; four are stately in their stride:
ESV.30: the lion, which is mightiest among beasts and does not turn back before any;
ESV.31: the strutting rooster,  the he-goat, and a king whose army is with him.
ESV.32: If you have been foolish, exalting yourself, or if you have been devising evil, put your hand on your mouth.
ESV.33: For pressing milk produces curds, pressing the nose produces blood, and pressing anger produces strife.

NASB.1: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, the oracle. The man declares to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal:
NASB.2: Surely I am more stupid than any man, And I do not have the understanding of a man.
NASB.3: Neither have I learned wisdom, Nor do I have the knowledge of the Holy One.
NASB.4: Who has ascended into heaven and descended? Who has gathered the wind in His fists? Who has wrapped the waters in His garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name or His son's name? Surely you know!
NASB.5: Every word of God is tested; He is a shield to those who take refuge in Him.
NASB.6: Do not add to His words Or He will reprove you, and you will be proved a liar.
NASB.7: Two things I asked of You, Do not refuse me before I die:
NASB.8: Keep deception and lies far from me, Give me neither poverty nor riches; Feed me with the food that is my portion,
NASB.9: That I not be full and deny [You] and say, 'Who is the LORD?' Or that I not be in want and steal, And profane the name of my God.
NASB.10: Do not slander a slave to his master, Or he will curse you and you will be found guilty.
NASB.11: There is a kind of [man] who curses his father And does not bless his mother.
NASB.12: There is a kind who is pure in his own eyes, Yet is not washed from his filthiness.
NASB.13: There is a kind-- oh how lofty are his eyes! And his eyelids are raised [in arrogance].
NASB.14: There is a kind of [man] whose teeth are [like] swords And his jaw teeth [like] knives, To devour the afflicted from the earth And the needy from among men.
NASB.15: The leech has two daughters, 'Give,' 'Give.' There are three things that will not be satisfied, Four that will not say, 'Enough':
NASB.16: Sheol, and the barren womb, Earth that is never satisfied with water, And fire that never says, 'Enough.'
NASB.17: The eye that mocks a father And scorns a mother, The ravens of the valley will pick it out, And the young eagles will eat it.
NASB.18: There are three things which are too wonderful for me, Four which I do not understand:
NASB.19: The way of an eagle in the sky, The way of a serpent on a rock, The way of a ship in the middle of the sea, And the way of a man with a maid.
NASB.20: This is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth, And says, 'I have done no wrong.'
NASB.21: Under three things the earth quakes, And under four, it cannot bear up:
NASB.22: Under a slave when he becomes king, And a fool when he is satisfied with food,
NASB.23: Under an unloved woman when she gets a husband, And a maidservant when she supplants her mistress.
NASB.24: Four things are small on the earth, But they are exceedingly wise:
NASB.25: The ants are not a strong people, But they prepare their food in the summer;
NASB.26: The shephanim are not mighty people, Yet they make their houses in the rocks;
NASB.27: The locusts have no king, Yet all of them go out in ranks;
NASB.28: The lizard you may grasp with the hands, Yet it is in kings' palaces.
NASB.29: There are three things which are stately in [their] march, Even four which are stately when they walk:
NASB.30: The lion [which] is mighty among beasts And does not retreat before any,
NASB.31: The strutting rooster, the male goat also, And a king [when his] army is with him.
NASB.32: If you have been foolish in exalting yourself Or if you have plotted [evil], [put your] hand on your mouth.
NASB.33: For the churning of milk produces butter, And pressing the nose brings forth blood; So the churning of anger produces strife.

NLT.1:
The sayings of Agur son of Jakeh contain this message. I am weary, O God;
I am weary and worn out, O God.
NLT.2:
I am too stupid to be human,
and I lack common sense.
NLT.3:
I have not mastered human wisdom,
nor do I know the Holy One.
NLT.4:
Who but God goes up to heaven and comes back down?
Who holds the wind in his fists?
Who wraps up the oceans in his cloak?
Who has created the whole wide world?
What is his name — and his son’s name?
Tell me if you know!
NLT.5:
Every word of God proves true.
He is a shield to all who come to him for protection.
NLT.6:
Do not add to his words,
or he may rebuke you and expose you as a liar.
NLT.7:
O God, I beg two favors from you;
let me have them before I die.
NLT.8:
First, help me never to tell a lie.
Second, give me neither poverty nor riches!
Give me just enough to satisfy my needs.
NLT.9:
For if I grow rich, I may deny you and say, “Who is the LORD?”
And if I am too poor, I may steal and thus insult God’s holy name.
NLT.10:
Never slander a worker to the employer,
or the person will curse you, and you will pay for it.
NLT.11:
Some people curse their father
and do not thank their mother.
NLT.12:
They are pure in their own eyes,
but they are filthy and unwashed.
NLT.13:
They look proudly around,
casting disdainful glances.
NLT.14:
They have teeth like swords
and fangs like knives.
They devour the poor from the earth
and the needy from among humanity.
NLT.15:
The leech has two suckers
that cry out, “More, more!”
There are three things that are never satisfied —
no, four that never say, “Enough!”:
NLT.16:
the grave,
the barren womb,
the thirsty desert,
the blazing fire.
NLT.17:
The eye that mocks a father
and despises a mother’s instructions
will be plucked out by ravens of the valley
and eaten by vultures.
NLT.18:
There are three things that amaze me —
no, four things that I don’t understand:
NLT.19:
how an eagle glides through the sky,
how a snake slithers on a rock,
how a ship navigates the ocean,
how a man loves a woman.
NLT.20:
An adulterous woman consumes a man,
then wipes her mouth and says, “What’s wrong with that?”
NLT.21:
There are three things that make the earth tremble —
no, four it cannot endure:
NLT.22:
a slave who becomes a king,
an overbearing fool who prospers,
NLT.23:
a bitter woman who finally gets a husband,
a servant girl who supplants her mistress.
NLT.24: There are four things on earth that are small but unusually wise:
NLT.25:
Ants — they aren’t strong,
but they store up food all summer.
NLT.26:
Hyraxes — they aren’t powerful,
but they make their homes among the rocks.
NLT.27:
Locusts — they have no king,
but they march in formation.
NLT.28:
Lizards — they are easy to catch,
but they are found even in kings’ palaces.
NLT.29:
There are three things that walk with stately stride —
no, four that strut about:
NLT.30: the lion, king of animals, who won’t turn aside for anything,
NLT.31:
the strutting rooster,
the male goat,
a king as he leads his army.
NLT.32:
If you have been a fool by being proud or plotting evil,
cover your mouth in shame.
NLT.33:
As the beating of cream yields butter
and striking the nose causes bleeding,
so stirring up anger causes quarrels.

WEB.1: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh; the revelation: the man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal:
WEB.2: “Surely I am the most ignorant man, and don’t have a man’s understanding.
WEB.3: I have not learned wisdom, neither do I have the knowledge of the Holy One.
WEB.4: Who has ascended up into heaven, and descended? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has bound the waters in his garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son’s name, if you know?
WEB.5: “Every word of God is flawless. He is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
WEB.6: Don’t you add to his words, lest he reprove you, and you be found a liar.
WEB.7: “Two things I have asked of you; don’t deny me before I die:
WEB.8: Remove far from me falsehood and lies. Give me neither poverty nor riches. Feed me with the food that is needful for me;
WEB.9: lest I be full, deny you, and say, ‘Who is Yahweh?’ or lest I be poor, and steal, and so dishonor the name of my God.
WEB.10: “Don’t slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you be held guilty.
WEB.11: There is a generation that curses their father, and doesn’t bless their mother.
WEB.12: There is a generation that is pure in their own eyes, yet are not washed from their filthiness.
WEB.13: There is a generation, oh how lofty are their eyes! Their eyelids are lifted up.
WEB.14: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords, and their jaws like knives, to devour the poor from the earth, and the needy from among men.
WEB.15: “The leach has two daughters: ‘Give, give.’ “There are three things that are never satisfied; four that don’t say, ‘Enough:’
WEB.16: Sheol,  the barren womb; the earth that is not satisfied with water; and the fire that doesn’t say, ‘Enough.’
WEB.17: “The eye that mocks at his father, and scorns obedience to his mother: the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, the young eagles shall eat it.
WEB.18: “There are three things which are too amazing for me, four which I don’t understand:
WEB.19: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent on a rock; the way of a ship in the middle of the sea; and the way of a man with a maiden.
WEB.20: “So is the way of an adulterous woman: she eats and wipes her mouth, and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’
WEB.21: “For three things the earth tremble, and under four, it can’t bear up:
WEB.22: For a servant when he is king; a fool when he is filled with food;
WEB.23: for an unloved woman when she is married; and a servant who is heir to her mistress.
WEB.24: “There are four things which are little on the earth, but they are exceedingly wise:
WEB.25: the ants are not a strong people, yet they provide their food in the summer.
WEB.26: The conies are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks.
WEB.27: The locusts have no king, yet they advance in ranks.
WEB.28: You can catch a lizard with your hands, yet it is in kings’ palaces.
WEB.29: “There are three things which are stately in their march, four which are stately in going:
WEB.30: The lion, which is mightiest among animals, and doesn’t turn away for any;
WEB.31: the greyhound, the male goat also; and the king against whom there is no rising up.
WEB.32: “If you have done foolishly in lifting up yourself, or if you have thought evil, put your hand over your mouth.
WEB.33: For as the churning of milk produces butter, and the wringing of the nose produces blood; so the forcing of wrath produces strife.”

CSB17.1:
The words of Agur son of Jakeh. The pronouncement. [1] ⓖ
The man’s oration to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal: [2]
CSB17.2:
I am more stupid than any other person, [3]
and I lack a human’s ability to understand. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
I have not gained wisdom,
and I have no knowledge of the Holy One. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
Who has gone up to heaven and come down? ⓙ
Who has gathered the wind in his hands?
Who has bound up the waters in a cloak? ⓚ
Who has established all the ends of the earth? ⓛ
What is his name,
and what is the name of his son —
if you know? ⓜ
CSB17.5:
Every word of God is pure; [4] ⓝ
he is a shield to those who take refuge in him. ⓐ
CSB17.6:
Don’t add to his words, ⓑ
or he will rebuke you, and you will be proved a liar. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
Two things I ask of you;
don’t deny them to me before I die:
CSB17.8:
Keep falsehood and deceitful words far from me.
Give me neither poverty nor wealth;
feed me with the food I need. ⓓ
CSB17.9:
Otherwise, I might have too much ⓔ
and deny you, ⓕ saying, “Who is the LORD?” ⓖ
or I might have nothing and steal, ⓗ
profaning [5] the name of my God. ⓘ
CSB17.10:
Don’t slander a servant to his master
or he will curse you, ⓙ and you will become guilty.
CSB17.11:
There is a generation that curses its father
and does not bless its mother. ⓚ
CSB17.12:
There is a generation that is pure in its own eyes, ⓛ
yet is not washed from its filth.
CSB17.13:
There is a generation — how haughty its eyes
and pretentious its looks. [6]
CSB17.14:
There is a generation whose teeth are swords, ⓜ
whose fangs are knives, ⓝ
devouring the oppressed from the land ⓐ
and the needy from among mankind.
CSB17.15:
The leech has two daughters: “Give, Give!”
Three things ⓑ are never satisfied;
four never say, “Enough!”:
CSB17.16:
Sheol; ⓒ a childless womb; ⓓ
earth, which is never satisfied with water;
and fire, which never says, “Enough!”
CSB17.17:
As for the eye that ridicules a father ⓔ
and despises obedience to a mother,
may ravens of the valley pluck it out
and young vultures eat it. ⓕ
CSB17.18:
Three things are too wondrous for me;
four I can’t understand:
CSB17.19:
the way of an eagle in the sky,
the way of a snake on a rock,
the way of a ship at sea,
and the way of a man with a young woman.
CSB17.20:
This is the way of an adulteress:
she eats and wipes her mouth
and says, “I’ve done nothing wrong.” ⓖ
CSB17.21:
The earth trembles under three things;
it cannot bear up under four:
CSB17.22:
a servant when he becomes king, ⓗ
a fool when he is stuffed with food,
CSB17.23:
an unloved woman when she marries, ⓘ
and a servant girl when she ousts her queen.
CSB17.24:
Four things on earth are small,
yet they are extremely wise:
CSB17.25:
ants are not a strong people,
yet they store up their food in the summer; ⓙ
CSB17.26:
hyraxes are not a mighty people,
yet they make their homes in the cliffs; ⓚ
CSB17.27:
locusts have no king,
yet all of them march in ranks; ⓛ
CSB17.28:
a lizard [7] can be caught in your hands,
yet it lives in kings’ palaces.
CSB17.29:
Three things are stately in their stride;
four are stately in their walk:
CSB17.30:
a lion, which is mightiest among beasts
and doesn’t retreat before anything;
CSB17.31:
a strutting rooster; [8] a goat;
and a king at the head of his army. [9]
CSB17.32:
If you have been foolish by exalting yourself
or if you’ve been scheming,
put your hand over your mouth. ⓜ
CSB17.33:
For the churning of milk produces butter,
and twisting a nose draws blood,
and stirring up anger produces strife. ⓝ

AMP.1:
The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, the oracle:
The man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and to Ucal:
AMP.2:
Surely I am more brutish and stupid than any man,
And I do not have the understanding of a man [for I do not know what I do not know].
AMP.3:
I have not learned [skillful and godly] wisdom,
Nor do I have knowledge of the Holy One [who is the source of wisdom].
AMP.4:
Who has ascended into heaven and descended?
Who has gathered the wind in His fists?
Who has bound the waters in His garment?
Who has established all the ends of the earth?
What is His name, and what is His Son's name?
Certainly you know!
AMP.5:
Every word of God is tested and refined [like silver];
He is a shield to those who trust and take refuge in Him.
AMP.6:
Do not add to His words,
Or He will reprove you, and you will be found a liar.
AMP.7:
Two things I have asked of You;
Do not deny them to me before I die:
AMP.8:
Keep deception and lies far from me;
Give me neither poverty nor riches;
Feed me with the food that is my portion,
AMP.9:
So that I will not be full and deny You and say, "Who is the LORD?"
Or that I will not be poor and steal,
And so profane the name of my God.
AMP.10:
Do not slander or malign a servant before his master [stay out of another's personal life],
Or he will curse you [for your interference], and you will be found guilty.
AMP.11:
There is a generation (class of people) that curses its father
And does not bless its mother.
AMP.12:
There is a generation (class of people) that is pure in its own eyes,
Yet is not washed from its filthiness.
AMP.13:
There is a generation (class of people)—oh, how lofty are their eyes!
And their eyelids are raised in arrogance.
AMP.14:
There is a generation (class of people) whose teeth are like swords
And whose jaw teeth are like knives,
To devour the afflicted from the earth
And the needy from among men.
AMP.15:
The leech has two daughters,
"Give, give!"
There are three things that are never satisfied,
Four that do not say, "It is enough":
AMP.16:
Sheol, and the barren womb,
Earth that is never satisfied with water,
And fire that never says, "It is enough."
AMP.17:
The eye that mocks a father
And scorns a mother,
The ravens of the valley will pick it out,
And the young vultures will devour it.
AMP.18:
There are three things which are too astounding and unexpectedly wonderful for me,
Four which I do not understand:
AMP.19:
The way of an eagle in the air,
The way of a serpent on a rock,
The way of a ship in the middle of the sea,
And the way of a man with a maid.
AMP.20:
This is the way of an adulterous woman:
She eats and wipes her mouth
And says, "I have done no wrong."
AMP.21:
Under three things the earth is disquieted and quakes,
And under four it cannot bear up:
AMP.22:
Under a servant when he reigns,
Under a [spiritually blind] fool when he is filled with food,
AMP.23:
Under an unloved woman when she gets married,
And under a maidservant when she supplants her mistress.
AMP.24:
There are four things that are small on the earth,
But they are exceedingly wise:
AMP.25:
The ants are not a strong people,
Yet they prepare their food in the summer;
AMP.26:
The shephanim are not a mighty folk,
Yet they make their houses in the rocks;
AMP.27:
The locusts have no king,
Yet all of them go out in groups;
AMP.28:
You may grasp the lizard with your hands,
Yet it is in kings' palaces.
AMP.29:
There are three things which are stately in step,
Even four which are stately in their stride:
AMP.30:
The lion, which is mighty among beasts
And does not turn back before any;
AMP.31:
The strutting rooster, the male goat also,
And the king when his army is with him.
AMP.32:
If you have foolishly exalted yourself,
Or if you have plotted evil, put your hand on your mouth.
AMP.33:
Surely the churning of milk produces butter,
And wringing the nose produces blood;
So the churning of anger produces strife.

MSG.1:
The skeptic swore, "There is no God!
No God!—I can do anything I want!
MSG.2:
I'm more animal than human;
so-called human intelligence escapes me.
MSG.3:
"I flunked 'wisdom.'
I see no evidence of a holy God.
MSG.4:
Has anyone ever seen Anyone
climb into Heaven and take charge?
grab the winds and control them?
gather the rains in his bucket?
stake out the ends of the earth?
Just tell me his name, tell me the names of his sons.
Come on now—tell me!"
MSG.5:
The believer replied, "Every promise of God proves true;
he protects everyone who runs to him for help.
MSG.6:
So don't second-guess him;
he might take you to task and show up your lies."
MSG.7:
And then he prayed, "God, I'm asking for two things
before I die; don't refuse me—
MSG.8:
Banish lies from my lips
and liars from my presence.
Give me enough food to live on,
neither too much nor too little.
MSG.9:
If I'm too full, I might get independent,
saying, 'God? Who needs him?'
If I'm poor, I might steal
and dishonor the name of my God."
MSG.10:
Don't blow the whistle on your fellow workers
behind their backs;
They'll accuse you of being underhanded,
and then you'll be the guilty one!
MSG.11:
Don't curse your father
or fail to bless your mother.
MSG.12:
Don't imagine yourself to be quite presentable
when you haven't had a bath in weeks.
MSG.13:
Don't be stuck-up
and think you're better than everyone else.
MSG.14:
Don't be greedy,
merciless and cruel as wolves,
Tearing into the poor and feasting on them,
shredding the needy to pieces only to discard them.
MSG.15:
A leech has twin daughters
named "Gimme" and "Gimme more."
Three things are never satisfied,
no, there are four that never say, "That's enough, thank you!"—
MSG.16:
hell,
a barren womb,
a parched land,
a forest fire.
MSG.17:
An eye that disdains a father
and despises a mother—
that eye will be plucked out by wild vultures
and consumed by young eagles.
MSG.18:
Three things amaze me,
no, four things I'll never understand—
MSG.19:
how an eagle flies so high in the sky,
how a snake glides over a rock,
how a ship navigates the ocean,
why adolescents act the way they do.
MSG.20:
Here's how a prostitute operates:
she has sex with her client,
Takes a bath,
then asks, "Who's next?"
MSG.21:
Three things are too much for even the earth to bear,
yes, four things shake its foundations—
MSG.22:
when the janitor becomes the boss,
when a fool gets rich,
MSG.23:
when a whore is voted "woman of the year,"
when a "girlfriend" replaces a faithful wife.
MSG.24:
There are four small creatures,
wisest of the wise they are—
MSG.25:
ants—frail as they are,
get plenty of food in for the winter;
MSG.26:
marmots—vulnerable as they are,
manage to arrange for rock-solid homes;
MSG.27:
locusts—leaderless insects,
yet they strip the field like an army regiment;
MSG.28:
lizards—easy enough to catch,
but they sneak past vigilant palace guards.
MSG.29:
There are three solemn dignitaries,
four that are impressive in their bearing—
MSG.30: a lion, king of the beasts, deferring to none;
MSG.31:
a rooster, proud and strutting;
a billy goat;
a head of state in stately procession.
MSG.32:
If you're dumb enough to call attention to yourself
by offending people and making rude gestures,
MSG.33:
Don't be surprised if someone bloodies your nose.
Churned milk turns into butter;
riled emotions turn into fist fights.

NIV.1:
Sayings of Agur
The sayings of Agur son of Jakeh—an oracle:
This man declared to Ithiel,
to Ithiel and to Ucal:
NIV.2:
“I am the most ignorant of men;
I do not have a man’s understanding.
NIV.3:
I have not learned wisdom,
nor have I knowledge of the Holy One.
NIV.4:
Who has gone up to heaven and come down?
Who has gathered up the wind in the hollow of his hands?
Who has wrapped up the waters in his cloak?
Who has established all the ends of the earth?
What is his name, and the name of his son?
Tell me if you know!
NIV.5:
“Every word of God is flawless;
he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
NIV.6:
Do not add to his words,
or he will rebuke you and prove you a liar.
NIV.7:
“Two things I ask of you, O Lord;
do not refuse me before I die:
NIV.8:
Keep falsehood and lies far from me;
give me neither poverty nor riches,
but give me only my daily bread.
NIV.9:
Otherwise, I may have too much and disown you
and say, ‘Who is the Lord?’
Or I may become poor and steal,
and so dishonor the name of my God.
NIV.10:
“Do not slander a servant to his master,
or he will curse you, and you will pay for it.
NIV.11:
“There are those who curse their fathers
and do not bless their mothers;
NIV.12:
those who are pure in their own eyes
and yet are not cleansed of their filth;
NIV.13:
those whose eyes are ever so haughty,
whose glances are so disdainful;
NIV.14:
those whose teeth are swords
and whose jaws are set with knives
to devour the poor from the earth,
the needy from among mankind.
NIV.15:
“The leech has two daughters.
‘Give! Give!’ they cry.
“There are three things that are never satisfied,
four that never say, ‘Enough!’:
NIV.16:
the grave, the barren womb,
land, which is never satisfied with water,
and fire, which never says, ‘Enough!’
NIV.17:
“The eye that mocks a father,
that scorns obedience to a mother,
will be pecked out by the ravens of the valley,
will be eaten by the vultures.
NIV.18:
“There are three things that are too amazing for me,
four that I do not understand:
NIV.19:
the way of an eagle in the sky,
the way of a snake on a rock,
the way of a ship on the high seas,
and the way of a man with a maiden.
NIV.20:
“This is the way of an adulteress:
She eats and wipes her mouth
and says, ‘I’ve done nothing wrong.’
NIV.21:
“Under three things the earth trembles,
under four it cannot bear up:
NIV.22:
a servant who becomes king,
a fool who is full of food,
NIV.23:
an unloved woman who is married,
and a maidservant who displaces her mistress.
NIV.24:
“Four things on earth are small,
yet they are extremely wise:
NIV.25:
Ants are creatures of little strength,
yet they store up their food in the summer;
NIV.26:
coneys are creatures of little power,
yet they make their home in the crags;
NIV.27:
locusts have no king,
yet they advance together in ranks;
NIV.28:
a lizard can be caught with the hand,
yet it is found in kings’ palaces.
NIV.29:
“There are three things that are stately in their stride,
four that move with stately bearing:
NIV.30:
a lion, mighty among beasts,
who retreats before nothing;
NIV.31:
a strutting rooster, a he-goat,
and a king with his army around him.
NIV.32:
“If you have played the fool and exalted yourself,
or if you have planned evil,
clap your hand over your mouth!
NIV.33:
For as churning the milk produces butter,
and as twisting the nose produces blood,
so stirring up anger produces strife.”

NRSVCE.1:
The words of Agur son of Jakeh. An oracle. Thus says the man: I am weary, O God,
I am weary, O God. How can I prevail?
NRSVCE.2:
Surely I am too stupid to be human;
I do not have human understanding.
NRSVCE.3:
I have not learned wisdom,
nor have I knowledge of the holy ones.
NRSVCE.4:
Who has ascended to heaven and come down?
Who has gathered the wind in the hollow of the hand?
Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment?
Who has established all the ends of the earth?
What is the person’s name?
And what is the name of the person’s child?
Surely you know!
NRSVCE.5:
Every word of God proves true;
he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
NRSVCE.6:
Do not add to his words,
or else he will rebuke you, and you will be found a liar.
NRSVCE.7:
Two things I ask of you;
do not deny them to me before I die:
NRSVCE.8:
Remove far from me falsehood and lying;
give me neither poverty nor riches;
feed me with the food that I need,
NRSVCE.9:
or I shall be full, and deny you,
and say, “Who is the LORD?”
or I shall be poor, and steal,
and profane the name of my God.
NRSVCE.10:
Do not slander a servant to a master,
or the servant will curse you, and you will be held guilty.
NRSVCE.11:
There are those who curse their fathers
and do not bless their mothers.
NRSVCE.12:
There are those who are pure in their own eyes
yet are not cleansed of their filthiness.
NRSVCE.13:
There are those — how lofty are their eyes,
how high their eyelids lift! —
NRSVCE.14:
there are those whose teeth are swords,
whose teeth are knives,
to devour the poor from off the earth,
the needy from among mortals.
NRSVCE.15:
The leech has two daughters;
“Give, give,” they cry.
Three things are never satisfied;
four never say, “Enough”:
NRSVCE.16:
Sheol, the barren womb,
the earth ever thirsty for water,
and the fire that never says, “Enough.”
NRSVCE.17:
The eye that mocks a father
and scorns to obey a mother
will be pecked out by the ravens of the valley
and eaten by the vultures.
NRSVCE.18:
Three things are too wonderful for me;
four I do not understand:
NRSVCE.19:
the way of an eagle in the sky,
the way of a snake on a rock,
the way of a ship on the high seas,
and the way of a man with a girl.
NRSVCE.20:
This is the way of an adulteress:
she eats, and wipes her mouth,
and says, “I have done no wrong.”
NRSVCE.21:
Under three things the earth trembles;
under four it cannot bear up:
NRSVCE.22:
a slave when he becomes king,
and a fool when glutted with food;
NRSVCE.23:
an unloved woman when she gets a husband,
and a maid when she succeeds her mistress.
NRSVCE.24:
Four things on earth are small,
yet they are exceedingly wise:
NRSVCE.25:
the ants are a people without strength,
yet they provide their food in the summer;
NRSVCE.26:
the badgers are a people without power,
yet they make their homes in the rocks;
NRSVCE.27:
the locusts have no king,
yet all of them march in rank;
NRSVCE.28:
the lizard can be grasped in the hand,
yet it is found in kings’ palaces.
NRSVCE.29:
Three things are stately in their stride;
four are stately in their gait:
NRSVCE.30:
the lion, which is mightiest among wild animals
and does not turn back before any;
NRSVCE.31:
the strutting rooster, the he-goat,
and a king striding before his people.
NRSVCE.32:
If you have been foolish, exalting yourself,
or if you have been devising evil,
put your hand on your mouth.
NRSVCE.33:
For as pressing milk produces curds,
and pressing the nose produces blood,
so pressing anger produces strife.

NET.1: The words of Agur, the son of Jakeh; an oracle: This man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and to Ukal:
NET.2: Surely I am more brutish than any other human being, and I do not have human understanding;
NET.3: I have not learned wisdom, nor do I have knowledge of the Holy One.
NET.4: Who has ascended into heaven, and then descended? Who has gathered up the winds in his fists? Who has bound up the waters in his cloak? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son’s name? — if you know!
NET.5: Every word of God is purified; he is like a shield for those who take refuge in him.
NET.6: Do not add to his words, lest he reprove you, and prove you to be a liar.
NET.7: Two things I ask from you; do not refuse me before I die:
NET.8: Remove falsehood and lies far from me; do not give me poverty or riches, feed me with my allotted portion of bread,
NET.9: lest I become satisfied and act deceptively and say, “Who is the LORD?” Or lest I become poor and steal and demean the name of my God.
NET.10: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you are found guilty.
NET.11: There is a generation who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
NET.12: There is a generation who are pure in their own eyes and yet are not washed from their filthiness.
NET.13: There is a generation whose eyes are so lofty, and whose eyelids are lifted up disdainfully.
NET.14: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords and whose molars are like knives to devour the poor from the earth and the needy from among the human race.
NET.15: The leech has two daughters: “Give! Give!” There are three things that are never satisfied, four that never say, “Enough” —
NET.16: the grave, the barren womb, land that is not satisfied with water, and fire that never says, “Enough!”
NET.17: The eye that mocks at a father and despises obeying a mother — the ravens of the valley will peck it out and the young vultures will eat it.
NET.18: There are three things that are too wonderful for me, four that I do not understand:
NET.19: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a snake on a rock, the way of a ship in the sea, and the way of a man with a woman.
NET.20: This is the way of an adulterous woman: she eats and wipes her mouth and says, “I have not done wrong.”
NET.21: Under three things the earth trembles, and under four things it cannot bear up:
NET.22: under a servant who becomes king, under a fool who is stuffed with food,
NET.23: under an unloved woman who is married, and under a female servant who dispossesses her mistress.
NET.24: There are four things on earth that are small, but they are exceedingly wise:
NET.25: ants are creatures with little strength, but they prepare their food in the summer;
NET.26: rock badgers are creatures with little power, but they make their homes in the crags;
NET.27: locusts have no king, but they all go forward by ranks;
NET.28: a lizard you can catch with the hand, but it gets into the palaces of the king.
NET.29: There are three things that are magnificent in their step, four things that move about magnificently:
NET.30: a lion, mightiest of the beasts, who does not retreat from anything;
NET.31: a strutting rooster, a male goat, and a king with his army around him.
NET.32: If you have done foolishly by exalting yourself or if you have planned evil, put your hand over your mouth!
NET.33: For as the churning of milk produces butter and as punching the nose produces blood, so stirring up anger produces strife.
